OBJECTIVE:
This report intends to document the findings of the visual & thermal inspection conducted by GarudaUAV for PGCIL.

The inspections determine the physical condition of the towers maintenance & insurance purposes.

The inspection helps understand the structural condition of the tower, its maintenance prospects and potentially problematic areas, which may lead to power outage or even grid failure. The inspections deliver a detailed visual assessment of the physical condition of the towers and their specific components which may lead to failure of the unit or pose hazard to employees, operators or the general public.

Our UAV-based inspection helps assess the condition of accessible and inaccessible components of the towers, identifies the deteriorated components and potentially hazardous conditions and helps pin-point anomalies which, with timely repair, can ensure the smooth functioning of the tower, line and grid.

We aim to detect anomalies such as:

1. Missing earth wire

2. Broken insulator, flash mark & Corrosion

3. Bird nests

4. Missing, damaged foundation or vegetation around foundation

5. Missing danger board & ICE number

6. Anti-climbing missing

7. Cable damage

8. Coating damage or breakdown

9. Hoarding, bird nest or kite string

10. Hotspot

Timely assessment and repair or maintenance ensures that requisite safety standards are maintained, the power line functions normally, T&D losses are kept to a minimum and consistent power supply is ensured to the end-users.
METHODOLOGY:
Our drone-based visual, thermal inspection & mapping is carried out in the following sequence:

1. Custom quad-copter drone is loaded with the appropriate flight plans.

2. The drone is equipped with:

▪ 48 MP camera (visual).

▪ Quadcoptor Drone Thermal Sensors  (09 mm focal length)

3. The calibration of thermal and visual camera is duly verified.

4. Before initiating the drone flight, ideal flight path is identified for unencumbered operations.

5. GarudaUAV’s proprietary mobile application helps create an automated flight plan for the site.

6. Area of interest is precisely identified and inspection carried out covering the entire area.

7. The images are carefully extracted post inspection.

8. These images are uploaded to our cloud platform where the inspection team runs automated services to identify anomalies and tag them. These are then compiled into a final report.
OPERATING STANDARDS:
Hotspot Criteria For Transmission lines:

Temperature Deviation Criterion: A hotspot is considered if the observed temperature is 10°C- 15°C higher than the ambient temperature.

Comparative Criterion: Additionally, a hotspot is also recognized if the observed temperature of an object is 10°C- 15°C higher than that of a similar/ adjacent component of different phase at the same location.

Classification of Hotspots (Based on ΔT Criteria - Qualitative and Quantitative):


Critical Hotspot :

i) High severity is allocated, if the observed temperature is greater than Ambient Temperature and 

ΔT > 5°C ( in case of Insulators) 

ΔT > 10°C ( in case of conductors/ Jumper wires) 

ii) If the temperature difference between similar objects in adjacent phases exceeds 10°C - 15°C.

ii) Very High severity is allocated, if the observed temperature is greater than Ambient Temperature and 

ΔT > 15°C ( in case of Insulators) 

ΔT > 15°C ( in case of conductors/ Jumper wires) 

Action: Immediate attention and corrective measures are necessary.

These criteria help in identifying and classifying hotspots in transmission lines, allowing for timely intervention to maintain the safe and efficient operation of the equipment.
ANALYSIS & RECOMMENDATIONS:
The inspection was conducted during May 2023. The ambient temperature was reported to be between 23°C and 34°C.

Various issues were found throughout the transmission line. These may cause failure of the line if not rectified in time. This report gives an insight into issues for the entire towers/poles. This information can be used to maintain an uninterrupted power supply and keep the power grid functional for a longer duration.

General Recommendations

1. Replace insulators with chipped spots, scratches on glazed coating, cracks / breakages or spots on insulators throughout.

2. Temperature shouldn’t exceed 100°C.

3. Fix the pins of insulators wherever needed.

4. Conductor should pass through the centre of the insulator.

5. Conductor joints should be properly arranged.

6. Scattered and messy connectors should be properly dressed.

7. Faulty conductors should be immediately replaced.

8. In case of sag in conductors, immediate tightening must be done.

9. Ensure proper placement of danger or caution boards.
